potential_missing.source_sentence: This exercise concerns a simplified system of a supermarket cash register.

The standard procedure of using a cash register is as follows: • A customer arrives at the checkout to pay for various items • The cashier records the bar code number of each item, as well as the quantity if it is greater than one.

• The customer selects his or her payment method: • Cash: the cashier takes the money from the customer and puts it in the cash register, the cash register indicates how much change the customer is to be given; • Cheque: the cashier verifies that the customer is financially solvent by sending a request to an authorisation centre via the cash register; • Credit card: a banking terminal forms part of the cash register.

potential_hallucinations.requirement: The system shall calculate and indicate the change to be given to the customer.
The system shall be initialised by the shop manager every morning for the day's operation.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall calculate and indicate the change to be given to the customer.
hallucinations.reason: This requirement is not mentioned in the source text. The cash register only indicates how much change the customer is to be given, but it does not calculate it.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall send a request for authorisation to an authorisation centre for credit card payments, according to the card type.
hallucinations.reason: This requirement is partially correct. The source text mentions that 'a banking terminal forms part of the cash register' and sends a request for authorisation, but it does not specify that it's based on the card type.

duplicates.requirement_a: The system shall display the price and description of each item.
duplicates.requirement_b: The system shall indicate the end of the sale when all purchases are recorded.
duplicates.reason: These two requirements are similar, but not identical. The first requirement is mentioned in the source text as 'The cash register displays the price of each item and its description.'

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall record the sale and print a receipt.
ambiguities.reason: This requirement is ambiguous. The source text mentions that 'the cash register records the sale' and 'prints a receipt', but it's not clear if these are two separate actions or one action with multiple effects.

Source text:
<<<
This exercise concerns a simplified system of a supermarket cash register. It is
inspired to a great extent by the case study of C. Larman’s first book (the Point-ofSale System), which formed the basis of the Valtech training about OOAD.
The standard procedure of using a cash register is as follows:
• A customer arrives at the checkout to pay for various items
• The cashier records the bar code number of each item, as well as the quantity if
it is greater than one.
• The cash register displays the price of each item and its description.
• When all the purchases are recorded, the cashier indicates the end of the sale.
• The cash register displays the total cost of the purchases.
• The customer selects his or her payment method:
• Cash: the cashier takes the money from the customer and puts it in the cash
register, the cash register indicates how much change the customer is to be
given;
• Cheque: the cashier verifies that the customer is financially solvent by
sending a request to an authorisation centre via the cash register;
• Credit card: a banking terminal forms part of the cash register. It sends a
request for authorisation to an authorisation centre, according to the card
type.
• The cash register records the sale and prints a receipt.
• The cashier gives the receipt to the customer.
Once the items have been entered, the customer can present money-off vouchers
for certain items to the cashier. When the payment transaction is finished, the cash
register sends the information on the number of items sold to the stock
management system.
Every morning, the shop manager initialises the cash registers for the day.
>>>

    "The system shall verify the customer's financial solvency by sending a request to an authorisation centre for cheque payments.",
    "The system shall send a request for authorisation to an authorisation centre for credit card payments, according to the card type.",
    "The system shall record the sale and print a receipt.",
    "The system shall send the information on the number of items sold to the stock management system after payment transaction is finished.",
    "The system shall be initialised by the shop manager every morning for the day's operation."
  ]
}
